caldav.notConfigured: Calendar sync has not been set up for this workspace yet.
caldav.title: Connect Your Calendar
caldav.subtitle: Follow these steps to see your appointments in your calendar app. This works with Apple Calendar (Mac, iPhone, iPad) and OneCalendar (Windows, Mac, mobile).
caldav.passwordHint: Click the button to copy your password. For security it is not shown here.
caldav.appleStep1: Open the **Calendar** app on your Mac
caldav.appleStep2: In the menu bar, go to **Calendar → Settings** (or press **⌘ ,**), then click the **Accounts** tab
caldav.appleStep3: Click the **+** button at the bottom left to add a new account
caldav.appleStep4: In the provider list, scroll down and select **Other CalDAV Account…**, then click **Continue**
caldav.appleStep6: Fill in the fields using the connection details above: enter your {{val:username:User Name}}, {{pwd:Password}}, {{val:server:Server Address}}, and {{val:path:Server Path}}
caldav.appleStep7: Set the {{val:port:Port}} and check **Use SSL** if shown above, then click **Sign In**
caldav.appleStep8: Your appointments will appear in the calendar sidebar — you can see them right away
caldav.appleImg1: caldav-apple-accounts.png — The Accounts settings panel — click + to add
caldav.appleImg2: caldav-apple-provider.png — Choose 'Other CalDAV Account…' at the bottom
caldav.appleImg3: caldav-apple-form.png — Set Account Type to Advanced and fill in your details
caldav.appleImg4: caldav-apple-result.png — Your appointments now appear in Apple Calendar
caldav.oneCalStep1: Download **OneCalendar** from [onecalendar.nl](https://www.onecalendar.nl/download/store) and open the app
caldav.oneCalStep2: Click the **+** button or go to **Settings** to add a new account
caldav.oneCalStep3: From the account type list, select **CalDAV**
caldav.oneCalStep4: In the **Server** field, paste the {{val:full_url:Full Server URL}} from above (use the copy button)
caldav.oneCalStep6: On the configure screen, make sure your calendar is toggled **on**, then click **Done**
caldav.oneCalStep7: Your appointments will now appear in OneCalendar
caldav.oneCalImg1: caldav-onecal-type.png — Select CalDAV from the account type list
caldav.oneCalImg2: caldav-onecal-form.png — Enter the server URL, username, and password
caldav.oneCalImg3: caldav-onecal-configure.png — Toggle your calendar on and click Done
caldav.oneCalImg4: caldav-onecal-result.png — Your appointments now appear in OneCalendar

email.pageSubtitle: Step-by-step guide for configuring your email account
email.notConfiguredDesc: Contact your administrator to set up the email configuration.
email.credentialsDesc: Use these credentials for all email services. The password is the same for all.
email.credentialNoteSuffix: — use it for both incoming and outgoing mail.
email.serverDesc: Use these settings in any email client — Thunderbird, Apple Mail, mobile apps, etc.
email.outlookDesc: Setup instructions for new and existing Outlook users — follow the screenshots below
email.otherDesc: Thunderbird, Apple Mail, Android, iPhone — use the manual server settings above

outlook.step1: Open Microsoft Outlook. On the welcome screen, enter your email address and click <0>Continue</0>.
outlook.step1Alt: Outlook welcome screen — enter email address
outlook.step2: <0>Existing Outlook users:</0> Go to <1>Settings → Account → Add Account</1>.
outlook.step3: Confirm by clicking <0>Add Account</0> to proceed.
outlook.step4: When asked how to connect, select <0>Synchronize directly with IMAP</0>.
outlook.step4Alt: Outlook account type selection — choose IMAP
outlook.step5: Enter your password. Outlook will automatically populate the IMAP and SMTP server settings.

other.step1: Open your email client and go to account settings.
other.step2: Choose to add a new account manually (do not use auto-detect).
other.step3: Enter your email address and password.
other.step4: For incoming mail, select IMAP and enter the server details from the table above.
other.step5: For outgoing mail, enter the SMTP server details with authentication enabled.

leadFallback.infoBanner: Fallback emails are sent automatically when a new lead is created. The lead must have an email address and the configuration must be enabled. Emails are sent via your area email configuration.
leadFallback.subjectPlaceholder: e.g. Thank you for your inquiry
leadFallback.htmlHint: Paste full HTML. Variables coming soon. The preview renders the email exactly as the recipient will see it.

leadFallback.missingFields: Subject and email content are required before this can go live.
